2604.11094.title: E2E-REME: Towards End-to-End Microservices Auto-Remediation via Experience-Simulation Reinforcement Fine-Tuning
2604.11094.paper_summary: Contemporary microservice systems continue to grow in scale and complexity, leading to increasingly frequent and costly failures. While recent LLM-based auto-remediation approaches have emerged, they primarily translate textual instructions into executable Ansible playbooks and rely on expert-crafted prompts, lacking runtime knowledge guidance and depending on large-scale general-purpose LLMs, which limits their accuracy and efficiency. We introduce \textit{End-to-End Microservice Remediation} (E2E-MR), a new task that requires directly generating executable playbooks from diagnosis reports to autonomously restore faulty systems. To enable rigorous evaluation, we build \textit{MicroRemed}, a benchmark that automates microservice deployment, failure injection, playbook execution, and post-repair verification. We further propose \textit{E2E-REME}, an end-to-end auto-remediation model trained via experience-simulation reinforcement fine-tuning. Experiments on public and industrial microservice platforms, compared with nine representative LLMs, show that E2E-REME achieves superior accuracy and efficiency.
2604.11094.paper_summary_zh: 當代微服務系統的規模和複雜性持續增長，導致故障的頻率和成本日益增加。雖然最近出現了基於大型語言模型（LLM）的自動修復方法，但它們主要將文本指令轉換為可執行的 Ansible playbook，並依賴專家編寫的提示，缺乏運行時知識指導，並依賴大規模通用 LLM，這限制了它們的準確性和效率。我們介紹了 \textit{End-to-End Microservice Remediation} (E2E-MR)，這是一項新的任務，要求直接從診斷報告生成可執行的 playbook，以自主恢復故障系統。為了實現嚴格的評估，我們構建了 \textit{MicroRemed}，這是一個基準，能自動化微服務部署、故障注入、playbook 執行和修復後驗證。我們進一步提出了 \textit{E2E-REME}，這是一個通過經驗模擬強化微調訓練的端到端自動修復模型。在公共和工業微服務平台上的實驗，與九個代表性的 LLM 進行比較，顯示 E2E-REME 在準確性和效率上均優於其他模型。
2604.11094.author: Lingzhe Zhang et.al.
2604.11094.authors: Lingzhe Zhang, Yunpeng Zhai, Tong Jia, Minghua He, Chiming Duan, Zhaoyang Liu, Bolin Ding, Ying Li
